channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Gombe Assembly Collapse: One dead, seven injured as Yahaya orders investigation
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The collapse of a section of the New Gombe State House of Assembly Complex under construction has left one worker dead and seven others sustaining varying degrees of injuries. The post Gombe Assembly Collapse: One dead, seven injured as Yahaya orders investigation appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Oyo Rescue: Obi celebrates freed pupils, demands security overhaul
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “I am greatly relieved and delighted to learn of the safe rescue of the schoolchildren who were kidnapped,” Obi said.

item.title: What’s next for Tony Elumelu?
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: For decades, Tony O. Elumelu, CFR, has remained one of Africa’s most influential bankers, investors, and philanthropists, shaping the continent’s financial landscape through visionary leadership and an unwavering belief in entrepreneurship. The post What’s next for Tony Elumelu? appeared first on T

item.title: More Thunder, Zeus Olympios and Docklands clash at Ascot
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: We have a busy day’s racing on Saturday with Flat action from Ascot and Chester, live on Sky Sports Racing...

item.title: Belgian GP: UK schedule and how to watch on Sky Sports
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The final double-header before Formula 1's summer break begins with the Belgian Grand Prix, live on Sky Sports, on July 17-19.

item.title: Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo’s growing influence in Africa
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: From King Mswati III, Hakainde Hichilema, Cyril Ramaphosa and William Ruto to Samia Suluhu Hassan, the founder of Intratrek Holdings has leveraged his political access to cultivate relationships with some of Africa’s most influential leaders.

item.title: Nigerian Nwadiukwu Ikenna arrested in Tokyo
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Investigators said Ikenna admitted to the allegation, telling authorities that he received a share of money from customers he referred to an eating establishment.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing the artificial intelligence company of orchestrating a coordinated effort to obtain confidential information related to its unreleased products, escalating tensions between two companies that were, until recently, close partners in the AI race. The

item.title: CCTV Footage: Keyamo demands apology, N25,000 fine from Peter Obi over airport parking claim
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The aviation minister says CCTV footage from Abuja airport contradicts Peter Obi’s claim that his vehicle was unjustly clamped, insisting the former presidential candidate should apologise to airport workers and pay the prescribed parking fine. item.title: Transfer: Ederson’s move from Atalanta to Man Utd collapses

item.title: Coordinated Security Operation Secures Release Of Kidnapped Oyo Pupils – Army
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Army has announced the rescue of 44 pupils and teachers abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State following a month-long, intelligence-led joint security operation that dismantled the kidnappers’ network across Oyo and other parts of the country. item.description: In what appears to be a last-ditch attempt to rescue the North from crippling insecurity, governors of the 19 northern states met in Kaduna midweek to announce the creation of the Northern Nigeria Security Trust Fund (NNSTF) to confront the region’s worsening security crisis. item.title: Shell Nears Project Execution Deal For Bonga North Oil Field Development
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Oil major Shell has said it is working towards the realisation of the Bonga North oil field in Nigeria. The firm is advancing the $5 billion Bonga North deep-water project to sustain and boost Nigeria’s oil production. The field is designed as a deep-water subsea tie-back to the existing Bonga

item.title: Independent Oil Producers Demand Review Of Oil Industry Tax Structure
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Independent Petroleum Producers Group (IPPG) is demanding a review of taxes and levies currently imposed on oil industry operators in Nigeria. The independent producer group observed that the Nigerian oil and gas industry remains the most taxed and levied in the country, and perhaps globally, wi

item.title: ‘Why foreign airlines rule Nigeria’s skies’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dickson Omobola Chief Commercial Officer of United Nigeria Airlines, Mr Adedayo Olawuyi, has fingered the dominance of foreign carriers in Nigeria’s aviation market to the weakness of domestic airlines, saying the country lacks strong local and regional operators capable of competing effectively.

Mudashiru Obasa, has expressed immense joy following the successful rescue of the kidnapped Oyo State schoolchildren and their teachers on the eve of Friday, July 10, 2026. item.title: Enugu Rangers Denies Simon’s Discrimination Claim
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) champions, Enugu Rangers, have denied claims by Super Eagles winger Moses Simon that he was not given a contract after a three-month trial because he was not Igbo. Simon, who currently plays for French Ligue 1 club Paris FC, made the claim in an interview with

item.title: Wemby signs multi-year contract with Spurs, tells fans 'I'm here to stay'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Victor Wembanyama committed his long-term future to the San Antonio Spurs on Friday, signing a reported five-year, $252 million rookie maximum contract extension after telling fans: "I'm here to stay. Whatever it takes."

item.title: Udom Emmanuel at 60: the arithmetic of a life well spent
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: There is a particular kind of leader who governs by ledger — who treats a state budget the way a careful banker treats a balance sheet, and who measures success not in applause but in outcomes that can be counted, built, or boarded. Udom Emmanuel, who turns sixty on July 11, is that kind of leader.

item.description: #SaturdayVibes #RestAndRecharge #CalmMindHappyHeart #LoveAndLight #PeacefulMomentsGood Morning...Todaywill be gentle. Let your heart rest. Have you carried so much?Dont worry cos God carries you. If the hate is loud,  His comfort is louder.He restores my soul.” — Psalm 23:3Stay strong and

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Recently, the torrential rains that flooded Nigeria’s commercial capital and brought disaster on residents, also brought relief to the sweltering heat in the city, as well as introduced business opportunities that mitigated losses. item.title: North-East Development Commission: Between promises and reality
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The North-East region has long grappled with economic marginalisation, weak infrastructure and limited social services. These conditions formed part of the broader environment in which the Boko Haram insurgency later emerged. Beyond the immediate destruction caused by the conflict, years of underinv

item.title: Beyond the Goals: Life lessons hidden in 90 minutes of football
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: She explains that if the first half of your marriage, business, life, or year is not going well, don’t write it off. The second half may surprise you The post Beyond the Goals: Life lessons hidden in 90 minutes of football appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: PFIPC saga: Whither the National Assembly?
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The current administration of President Bola Tinubu is presently reeling from a broadside salvo of a scandal that questions its handling of a phantom agency that, though not created by law, has been operating illegally and enjoys the official perquisites of office. The perquisites include the alloca

item.title: On development funding for Nigerian CSOs in post-USAID world
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Prior to the exit of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) from the Nigerian development and humanitarian programming space in January 2025, the Mission was the country’s largest international development funder, outstripping all other bilateral organisations, including the

item.title: Patriot missile license a 'long-term' investment for Ukraine – but can it afford the wait?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s surprise announcement that he will approve a license for Ukraine to produce its own Patriot missile interceptors signals a potential breakthrough for Kyiv, though experts caution that it will not translate into an immediate fix.

item.title: Haaland has advantage over Man City team-mates
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Micah Richards, Wayne Rooney and Cesar Azpilicueta look ahead to England's match against Norway and feel that Erling Haaland will have an advantage over his Manchester City team-mates in the match.

item.title: Oga Wike, listen to FCT teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: If there’s any set of basic school teachers in the country that have suffered much frustration and mistreatment in the pursuit of their fundamental labour rights in recent years, the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) teachers are second to none. On a comparative national scale, they appear to have gon

item.title: Top 5 kit brands dominating the FIFA 2026 World Cup
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The FIFA 2026 World Cup is not only showcasing the world’s best footballers but also the biggest battle among global read more Top 5 kit brands dominating the FIFA 2026 World Cup

item.title: Live: Trump says US will 'completely decimate' Iran if it attempts to assassinate him
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday the United States would "completely decimate" Iran if it attempted or succeeded in assassinating the sitting president. "Orders have already been given, and the US Military is ready, willing, and able, for a one year period of time, subject to extension, to complet

item.title: ‘PFIPC’ and IMF’s rebuke
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Nigeria’s media space has been excited in the past two weeks. This is courtesy of two lurid controversies that verge on the scandalous and surreal, and which seized its upper reaches in near seismic proportion. The first saga is the convoluted one of a pseudo government agency – the Presidenti

item.title: FG targets 30% cut in cancer burden by 2030
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The federal government has unveiled a National Cancer Control Plan aimed at reducing Nigeria’s cancer burden by 30 per cent by 2030, while acknowledging that funding for cancer care remains inadequate. Minister of State for Health and Social Welfare, Dr Iziaq Adekunle Salako, disclosed this on Frida

item.title: England set for open-air Miami heat - but do Norway hold the edge?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England will head into unknown territory at this World Cup when they face Norway in Miami - playing in searing temperatures.

item.title: World Cup Miss: “Entire NFF Board Needs To Go” – Mikel Obi
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Former Nigeria captain, Mikel Obi has described Nigeria’s failure to qualify for the ongoing World Cup as a ‘hammer blow’. Speaking on the Obi One Podcast, Mikel said, “I feel for the people of my country. I feel for my people in Nigeria because they are going to be devastated right now. I just feel
